issues.criterion: design_notes.c10_subcheck_design_and_join_bounding_finding REV2's group-closing condition (a): a physical line closes a group ONLY if it ends in `.`/`!`/`?` optionally followed by a closing quote/paren/emphasis char (`'`,`"`,`)`,`]`,`*`) and then only whitespace. Probed against realistic HTML-styled prose -- specifically the ACTUAL formatting convention docs/guide/index.html itself already uses throughout its body -- to test the NEW residual risk the removed line-cap introduces (task item 3).
issues.evidence:
docs/guide/index.html's every `

...

` sentence in the shipped file ends its physical line in `.

` (or `.`, etc.), never in a bare `.` (or `.` followed only by a quote/paren/asterisk). Since `<`,`/`,`p`,`>` are NOT in the join's allowed closing-character bracket class, NONE of these lines ever satisfy close condition (a) -- the ONLY remaining close condition left for this file's actual markup is a blank line. Directly verified on the real shipped file: the entire 'Module Reference' `
` block (docs/guide/index.html lines 513-567, spanning six distinct `
` cards for standard/, methodology/, registry/, templates/, schema/, and tools/) collapses into ONE single merged join-group today, because none of its 13 `.

`-terminated lines in that span close a group and the next blank line does not occur until line 570 (confirmed by direct count: `awk 'NR==513,NR==570' docs/guide/index.html | grep -c '\.

$'` -> 13, and by re-running the rev2 oracle join against the real file, whose printed joined-group output shows this entire six-card block as a single output line). This is currently benign ONLY because no individual card's own paragraph happens to enumerate exactly {standard/, templates/, schema/} without tools/ -- it is a content accident, not a soundness guarantee. To PROVE this is a live false-pass mechanism (not merely a benign coincidence), a fixture mirroring the guide's own exact `

...

`, no-intervening-blank-line convention was built (tools/fixtures-equivalent oracle input, not touching any shipped file): a stale enumeration paragraph ending `...only.

` immediately followed (same block, no blank line) by an unrelated, separately-`.

`-terminated tools/-mentioning paragraph. Oracle result: `[C10-subcheck1] PASS payload-set` (EXIT:0) -- the two paragraphs are silently merged into one group containing all four directory tokens, and the stale enumeration is never caught. This is the EXACT false-pass class rev0/rev1/rev2 have each been fixing for markdown prose ( `.` at physical-line-end ), reappearing, unfixed, for HTML prose ( `.

` at physical-line-end ) -- and it is directly and concretely anchored to the real shipped corpus's only `.html` file, not a contrived string: docs/guide/index.html uses this exact `.

`-per-sentence, sparse-blank-line convention pervasively (only 66 blank lines total across 720 lines, overwhelmingly at section/div boundaries, not between paragraphs). This file is swept by C10 sub-check (1) in BOTH the default-mode 10-check sweep (via reuse of c9_default_files, per design_notes.three_mode_pinning) AND is the intended real-world target of the new `*.html` --target case this very contract adds (AT6/version_drift.html precedent) -- so any future edit adding one more clause, list item, or card to any of the guide's several large un-blank-line-separated HTML blocks (the identical shape of edit this contract's own NDEBT-003(a) fix already makes to this same file's key-documents list) that happens to introduce a genuinely-stale enumeration would sail through C10 completely undetected, exactly the silent-regression class this phase's durability thesis and this contract's own stated purpose ('no stale 3-dir enumeration survives ANYWHERE') exist to prevent. Markdown-only fixtures are independently confirmed unaffected (re-ran the rev0/rev1/rev2 markdown co-sentence probe -- still correctly FAILs) -- this is a NEW, distinct, HTML-specific defect, not a regression of the already-fixed markdown behavior.
issues.required_fix:
Extend the join's group-closing recognition so an HTML-styled sentence terminator is also recognized as closing a group -- e.g. permit the terminal-punctuation bracket expression to be followed by a closing HTML tag (`

`, ``, ``, ``, etc.) in addition to the current quote/paren/emphasis set, or add an explicit supplementary close condition for a line ending in a block-level closing tag. Add a dedicated regression fixture styled directly on docs/guide/index.html's own actual formatting (multiple `

...

` paragraphs, no intervening blank line) proving a stale 3-dir enumeration ending `.

` is still caught even when immediately followed, same HTML block, by an unrelated tools/-mentioning `

...

` paragraph. Re-verify this fix does not resurrect the rev0 markdown false-pass or either rev1 false-negative, and re-run the full default+payload corpus non-breaking gate.

missing_acceptance_coverage:
No verification entry or fixture in contract 027 (rev2) tests an HTML-styled stale 3-directory enumeration (a sentence ending in `.

` rather than a bare `.`) sharing an un-blank-line-separated HTML block with a separately-`.

`-terminated, unrelated tools/-mentioning paragraph -- see issues[0]. This is a reproducible, silent miss of genuine drift specifically in the file format (`.html`) this contract's own new `*.html` --target case (and the default-mode sweep, via docs/guide/index.html) is designed to check.
observations: REV1->REV2 DELTA CONFIRMED SOUND for markdown prose: independently built an oracle strictly from design_notes' rev2 prose (frontmatter-strip -> cap-free awk forward pass, closing a group ONLY at a line-end `.`/`!`/`?` optionally trailing a closing quote/paren/emphasis char, or a blank line -- `;`/`:` never close -- calling the REAL unmodified vlib_no_stale_payload once per file). (1) rev0 false-pass STILL closed: reproduced the co-located-sentence probe (period-terminated stale-3-dir sentence + period-terminated unrelated tools/ sentence, same paragraph, separate physical lines) -- oracle output shows the two sentences correctly split into TWO separate groups, `[C10-subcheck1] FAIL payload-set` fires on the first group alone. (2) rev1 false-negative (a) (hard 3-line cap fragmenting a long unpunctuated wrap) now CAUGHT: reproduced a >3-physical-line stale enumeration with no terminator until the final line -- correctly FAILs (previously PASSed under rev1). (3) A realistic bulleted-list variant of the same shape (4 physical lines, no cap to fragment it) also correctly FAILs, confirming the fix is general, not fixture-shape-specific. (4) rev1 false-negative (b) (mid-enumeration `;`-as-terminator) now CAUGHT: reproduced the exact `;`-broken two-line enumeration -- correctly FAILs (previously PASSed under rev1), confirming `;`/`:` genuinely no longer force-close a group.

NON-BREAKING GATE RE-VERIFIED EXACTLY as claimed: reconstructed the real 38-file default-mode set (build_shipped_md_set union docs/guide/index.html) and the real 21-file payload-mode set (build_payload_md_set) from tools/validate.sh's own function bodies, and ran the rev2 oracle join + real vlib_no_stale_payload against every file. WITHOUT the standard/GIP.md content fix: exactly 1 failure in default mode (standard/GIP.md itself) and exactly 1 in payload mode (same file, also in that set) -- matching the contract's claimed 'exactly one failure ... proving the fix is genuinely forced'. WITH the GIP.md fix applied in-memory only (never touching the real file): 0 failures in BOTH modes. Independently confirmed the GIP.md fix's own close mechanism: item 3's own four-physical-line span ('3. **Detecting local mutation.**...well-intentioned.') closes strictly at its own terminal period, WITHOUT reaching into item 4's text -- matching design_notes.pre_existing_finding_gip's claim exactly, with no cap involved at all.

Spot-confirmed sound: docs/architecture/ADR-001's wrapped 4-dir enumeration PASSes; docs/guide/index.html's `

` enumeration (lines 426-428 area) and its ASCII `

` directory-tree block both PASS (the tree's group legitimately contains all four directory tokens together, confirmed by direct inspection of the joined output) -- though it is now understood (see issues[0]) that this PASS, and the guide's overall 0-failure result, rest partly on a large-scale merge mechanism (whole un-blank-line-separated HTML blocks collapsing into one group) that is benign only by current-content coincidence, not by structural guarantee.

REGEX CORRECTNESS CONFIRMED (task item 6): tested the bracket expression `[.!?][]*'")]*[[:space:]]*$` (as literally given) directly in real GNU awk against lines ending in `.`,`!`,`?`, each bare or followed by `"`,`'`,`)`,`]`,`*`, plus trailing whitespace -- all correctly MATCH; lines ending in `;` or `:` correctly do NOT match. This is a well-formed POSIX/ERE bracket expression with no bug that would mis-close or never-close a group for markdown-style prose. Separately verified design_notes' own literally-transcribed JSON string (which contains an extra backslash before the embedded quote, `[.!?][]*'\"')]*[[:space:]]*$`) still produces IDENTICAL matching behavior in real gawk -- only a non-fatal 'regexp escape sequence' warning on stderr, no incorrect match/non-match on any tested input -- confirming the carried-forward non-blocking note from the rev1 Mode-A validator review remains accurate and non-blocking at rev2 as well.

VACUOUS PRE-PASS CHECK (task item 7): ran all 25 real verification commands from .agent/contracts/027.json (rev2; 21->25, three new entries [13]-[16] added for the longwrap/semicolon fixtures and their anti-cheat pairs, one existing index shift) against the CURRENT unimplemented tree. Exactly 4 entries legitimately PASS pre-implementation for correct 'nothing broke / nothing out-of-scope yet' reasons (index 2: AT3 exit-0 regression: RC=0; index 8: AT9 scope guard: PASS; index 9: AT10 C1-C9-still-pass regression: PASS; index 24: non-goal enforcement: PASS). All 21 remaining entries correctly FAIL on the unimplemented tree for content-specific reasons (missing check_c10_consistency function, missing fixture files, unfixed guide/methodology/GIP content, no `*.html` target case yet). No vacuous pre-pass detected.

P3/EXTERNAL-ANCHOR-RULE/SCOPE spot-checks all re-confirmed unchanged and sound: CONTEXT.md independently re-confirmed to contain neither canonical discovery-order label; docs/guide/index.html's meta and footer version anchors independently re-confirmed at 0.5.1 (unchanged); tools/validate.sh's target switch independently re-confirmed to have no existing `*.html)` case (confirming the new case is genuinely additive); vlib_scope_guard and vlib_version_increased independently re-confirmed present in tools/verify_lib.sh.

The new stale_payload_longwrap.md and stale_payload_semicolon.md fixture directions described in scope.files_create genuinely prove their claimed direction when reconstructed from their described content (both correctly FAIL under the rev2 oracle join), consistent with the contract's own dry-run claims.

final_verdict.summary:
REJECTED at revision 2. The two rev1 blockers (the hard 3-physical-line cap fragmenting long unpunctuated wraps, and `;`/`:` treated as an unconditional sentence terminator prematurely force-closing mid-enumeration) ARE genuinely and correctly fixed by rev2's cap-free, `.`/`!`/`?`-only join -- independently re-verified via oracle dry run against reconstructed longwrap and bulleted-list fixtures (now caught) and a semicolon-broken fixture (now caught), and the rev0 co-located-sentence false-pass remains correctly closed. The full 38-file default corpus and 21-file payload corpus were independently reconstructed from tools/validate.sh's own build_shipped_md_set/build_payload_md_set and confirmed non-breaking exactly as claimed: 0 sub-check-1 failures in both modes with the standard/GIP.md content fix applied, and exactly 1 failure (GIP.md itself) in each mode without it -- proving the fix is genuinely forced. The suggested regex is a well-formed, correctly-matching POSIX ERE for markdown prose. However, adversarially probing the specific NEW residual risk the cap's removal introduces (an uncapped join can merge everything up to the next terminator) surfaced a genuine, corpus-anchored defect distinct from anything previously reviewed: docs/guide/index.html -- the one real .html file in the corpus, swept by C10 both in the default 10-check sweep and as the intended real-world target of this contract's own new `*.html` --target case -- writes every one of its `

` sentences ending in `.

`, never a bare `.`, so NONE of them ever satisfy the join's terminal-punctuation close condition; the only remaining close condition for this file's actual markup is a blank line, and blank lines are sparse (66 across 720 lines, mostly at section boundaries). This is directly demonstrated on the real file: its 'Module Reference' card-grid block (lines 513-567, six distinct article cards) collapses into ONE merged join-group today, purely by luck not containing a stale enumeration. A fixture faithfully reproducing this exact real-file convention (a stale 3-dir `

...only.

` paragraph immediately followed, no blank line, by an unrelated tools/-mentioning `

...only.

` paragraph) reproduces a genuine false-PASS (EXIT:0) via the oracle. This is not a contrived, never-in-docs string -- it is the shipped guide's own default prose formatting convention, and the exact class of edit that would trigger it (adding one more card/clause to an existing un-blank-line-separated HTML block) is the same shape of edit this very contract's own NDEBT-003(a) fix already makes to this same file. Required before re-review: extend the join's close condition to also recognize an HTML block-level closing tag (e.g. `

`, ``) immediately after the terminal punctuation (or an equivalent supplementary HTML-aware close rule), with a dedicated regression fixture proving an HTML-styled stale enumeration is still caught when immediately adjacent (same block, no blank line) to an unrelated tools/ mention -- then re-verify the full non-breaking gate and both prior fixes are undisturbed. All other design elements (P3 discovery-order scoping, the External Anchor Rule, the html-target wiring itself, the scope guard, the 25 verification commands' resistance to vacuous pre-passing on the unimplemented tree, and both new markdown fixtures' proven directions) are independently re-confirmed sound at rev2 and require no changes.
